title: Nested Conditionals and Ternary Practice
instructions: Practice nested conditionals and ternary operators. Write clean, readable conditional logic.
starterCode:
def get_ticket_price(age, is_student):
    """Calculate ticket price based on age and student status
    Base: $20, Children (under 12): $10, Seniors (65+): $15, Students: $12"""
    pass

def check_login(username, password, is_active):
    """Check login credentials
    Return 'success', 'inactive', 'invalid_username', or 'invalid_password'"""
    pass

def get_shipping_cost(weight, is_express):
    """Calculate shipping: standard $5 (<=5kg), $10 (<=20kg), $15 (>20kg)
    Express adds 50% to cost"""
    pass
hints: Check conditions in order from most specific to most general
Use ternary operator for simple two-way choices
Guard clauses help flatten nested logic

testCases.description: get_ticket_price should return 10 for children under 12
testCases.validation: assert get_ticket_price(8, False) == 10, 'Children under 12 should pay $10'

testCases.description: get_ticket_price should return 12 for students and 20 for regular adults
testCases.validation: assert get_ticket_price(25, True) == 12 and get_ticket_price(25, False) == 20, 'Students pay $12, regular adults pay $20'

testCases.description: check_login should return success for valid admin credentials
testCases.validation: assert check_login('admin', 'secret', True) == 'success', 'Valid credentials with active account should succeed'

testCases.description: check_login should return appropriate error messages for invalid inputs
testCases.validation: assert check_login('user', 'secret', True) == 'invalid_username' and check_login('admin', 'wrong', True) == 'invalid_password' and check_login('admin', 'secret', False) == 'inactive', 'Should return correct error for each failure case'

testCases.description: get_shipping_cost should calculate correct cost with express option
testCases.validation: assert get_shipping_cost(3, False) == 5 and get_shipping_cost(3, True) == 7.5 and get_shipping_cost(15, False) == 10 and get_shipping_cost(25, True) == 22.5, 'Should apply weight tiers and 50% express surcharge'

description: Practice nested conditionals and ternary operators by implementing a ticket pricing system with age-based tiers, a login validation flow with multiple failure modes, and a shipping cost calculator with weight brackets and express surcharge. Write clean, readable conditional logic using if-elif-else chains and guard clauses.
